title: Formal Analysis of the Trivy / TeamPCP GitHub Actions Supply-Chain Attack (CVE-2026-33634)
description: A formally verified, quantitative reconstruction of the March-2026 Trivy / TeamPCP GitHub Actions supply-chain compromise (CVE-2026-33634). Three validation layers: (1) a static analyzer over a 189-workflow corpus that grounds the model's assumptions; (2) a TLA+/TLC incident-reconstruction model proving which mitigations close the attack (per-pipeline isolation, a refinement relation, and the residual attack surface); (3) a PRISM probabilistic model for compromise probabilities, expected times, a two-stage npm cascade, and closed-form parametric results, calibrated against public malicious-package data. Includes an LLM-driven mitigation search whose every candidate is verified by the PRISM oracle. The software and formal-methods artifacts are licensed Apache-2.0; the paper text is licensed CC-BY-4.0.
